title: Craig Greenhill

{{short description|Australia international rugby league footballer}} {{Use Australian English|date=July 2013}} {{Use dmy dates|date=June 2023}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Craig Greenhill |fullname = Craig Greenhill |image = |caption = |position = {{Rlp|SR|PR}} |birth_date = {{birth date and age|1972|2|14|df=yes}} |birth_place = [[Queensland]], Australia |height = {{convert|186|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|110|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} |club1 = [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla Sharks]] |year1start = 1994 |year1end = 98 |appearances1 = 55 |tries1 = 4 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 16 |club2 = [[Penrith Panthers]] |year2start = 1999 |year2end = 01 |appearances2 = 61 |tries2 = 0 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 0 |club3 = [[Hull F.C.]] |year3start = 2002 |year3end = 03 |appearances3 = 60 |tries3 = 3 |goals3 = 1 |fieldgoals3 = 0 |points3 = 12 |club4 = [[Castleford Tigers]] |year4start = 2004 |year4end = |appearances4 = 28 |tries4 = 1 |goals4 = 0 |fieldgoals4 = 0 |points4 = 4 |teamA = [[Queensland Maroons|Queensland]] |yearAstart = 1996 |yearAend = 00 |appearancesA = 6 |triesA = 0 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 0 |teamB = [[Super League Tri-series#Queensland|Queensland (SL)]] |yearBstart = 1997 |yearBend = |appearancesB = 1 |triesB = 0 |goalsB = 0 |fieldgoalsB = 0 |pointsB = 0 |teamC = [[Super League Test series|Australia (SL)]] |yearCstart = 1997 |yearCend = |appearancesC = 3 |triesC = 0 |goalsC = 0 |fieldgoalsC = 0 |pointsC = 0 |retired = yes |source = {{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/craig-greenhill/summary.html|title= Craig Greenhill – Career Stats & Summary – Rugby League Project|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2019-11-06}} |updated = 6 November 2019 }} '''Craig Greenhill''' (born {{birth date|1972|2|14|df=yes}}) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He represented [[Queensland Maroons]] in [[Rugby League State of Origin|State of Origin]], as a {{rlp|sr}}. ==Playing career== Greenhill made his first grade debut for Cronulla in round 2 1995 against rival [[St. George Dragons|St. George]] at [[Kogarah Oval]]. Greenhill played in Cronulla's semi-final defeat against the [[Newcastle Knights]] that year. In the [[1996 ARL season]], Greenhill played in Cronulla's preliminary final loss against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] which finished 0–24 at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]]. The following year in 1997, Cronulla joined the rival Super League competition during the [[Super League war]].Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players. (1995 edition) {{ISBN|1875169571}} In the 1997 post season, Greenhill was selected to play for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] from the interchange bench in two matches of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/a-history-of-heartbreak-cronulla-sharks-tale-of-woe-in-nrl-finals-20160916-grib48.html/|title=A history of heartbreak: Cronulla Sharks' tale of woe in NRL finals|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=17 September 2016 }} Greenhill played in Cronulla's grand final defeat against the [[Brisbane Broncos]]. Greenhill then departed Cronulla at the end of 1998 and signed with Penrith. In the [[2000 NRL season]], Penrith finished 5th after enjoying one of their best seasons since winning the premiership in 1991. Greenhill played in both finals games for Penrith which ended in defeat. Greenhill's final year in the [[NRL]] as a player came in the [[2001 NRL season]] where Penrith finished last on the table. His final game was in round 26 2001 where Penrith were defeated 18–60 by Newcastle who would go on to win the premiership that season. After leaving Penrith, Greenhill finished his career in England with [[Super League]] teams Hull F.C. and [[Castleford Tigers]] ([[List of Castleford Tigers players|Heritage No. 805]]). ==Trivia== Craig Greenhill was the first player to be sent off in State of Origin football.
Appeared in ''[[Conan the Barbarian (1982 film)|Conan the Barbarian]]'' as an extra; a young boy in Conan's tribe. Greenhill is attempting to become a golf professional in the senior ranks-he will be eligible in 2022. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20090201083149/http://qrl.com.au/qrl_history/qld_rep_g.php Queensland representatives at qrl.com.au] {{DEFAULTSORT:Greenhill, Craig}} [[Category:1972 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Australian expatriate rugby league players in England]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Penrith Panthers players]] [[Category:Queensland Rugby League State of Origin players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Queensland]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:Wynnum Manly Seagulls players]] [[Category:Place of birth missing (living people)]] {{Australia-rugbyleague-bio-1970s-stub}}
